supporting_works.title: The pharmacopoeia universalis or, complete encyclopedia of the materia medica; contained in the pharmacopoeias of London, Edinburgh, and Dublin as well as of all those of Europe and America ; and of the dispensatories, formularies and chemical works ... With an appendix ... / by A. J. L. Jourdan : Edited by J. Rennie.

The Book of the household, or, Family dictionary of everything connected with housekeeping and domestic medicine : with the treatment of children, management of the sick room, the sanitary improvements of the dwelling, the duties of servants, and full information on all other subjects relating to personal and domestic comfort  / compiled by competent persons, under the superintendence of an association of heads of families and men of science.

Cooley's cyclopaedia of practical receipts and collateral information in the arts, manufactures, professions, and trades, including medicine, pharmacy, hygiene, and domestic economy : designed as a comprehensive supplement to the pharmacopoeia and general book of reference for the manufacturer, tradesman, amateur, and heads of families.
The dispensatory of the United States of America / by Geo. B. Wood and Franklin Bache.

The book of prescriptions : containing upwards of 3000 prescriptions collected from the practice of the most eminent physicians and surgeons, English and foreign comprising also a compendious history of the materia medica, lists of the doses of all official or established preparations and an index of diseases and remedies / by Henry Beasley.
A guide to health through the various stages of life : ... the whole illustrated with useful annotations
supporting_works.creator: Lynch, Bernard, -1745.

supporting_works.title: An encyclopaedia of domestic economy comprising such subjects as are most immediately connected with housekeeping : as, the construction of domestic edifices ... duties of servants, the general account of the animal and vegetable substances used as food ... making bread ... preservation of health ; domestic medicine &c. &c. / by Thomas webster ; assisted by the late Mrs. Parkes.
The domestic oracle, or, a complete system of modern cookery and family economy ... To the whole are added ... monitions for the preservation of health and ... receipts / [Alexander Murray].

A manual of materia medica and pharmacy : comprising a concise description of the articles used in medicine, their physical and chemical properties, the botanical characters of the medicinal plants, and the formulae for the principal officinal preparations of the American, Parisian, London, Dublin, Edinburgh, &c pharmacopoeiae : with observations on the proper mode of combining and administering remedies
supporting_works.creator: Milne-Edwards, H. (Henri), 1800-1885.

supporting_works.title: Fenner's working formulae : a hand-book of the old and the new pharmacopoeias, containing weight and measure standards, working processes, working formulae, the medicinal properties and doses of drugs and their preparations, and being a comparison of the 1870 with the 1880 Pharmacopoeia : with remarks, suggestions, original processes and formulae, and other matter of interest and value to druggists and physicians
supporting_works.creator: Fenner, B. (Byron), 1844-1926 author.

retrieval_text: Orange is a culinary medicinal plant profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 564 works and 2614 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Orange, Oranges, Orange Flowers, Orange Flower, Orange Berries, Orange Berry. Common plant-part co-mentions include Flower, Root, Fruit, Bark, Leaf, Gum. Common preparation co-mentions include Juice, Syrup, Oil, Tea, Powder, Cordial. Common condition co-mentions include Fever, Cold or catarrh, Vomiting or nausea, Diarrhoea or looseness, Epilepsy or convulsions, Cough. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Child-use caution, Bleeding caution, Toxicity watch, Fatal-risk warning. Representative source works include The pharmacopoeia universalis or, complete encyclopedia of the materia medica; contained in the pharmacopoeias of London, Edinburgh, and Dublin as well as of all those of Europe and America ; and of the dispensatories, formularies and chemical works ... With an appendix ... / by A. J. L. Jourdan : Edited by J. Rennie.; The Book of the household, or, Family dictionary of everything connected with housekeeping and domestic medicine : with the treatment of children, management of the sick room, the sanitary improvements of the dwelling, the duties of servants, and full information on all other subjects relating to personal and domestic comfort  / compiled by competent persons, under the superintendence of an association of heads of families and men of science.; Cooley's cyclopaedia of practical receipts and collateral information in the arts, manufactures, professions, and trades, including medicine, pharmacy, hygiene, and domestic economy : designed as a comprehensive supplement to the pharmacopoeia and general book of reference for the manufacturer, tradesman, amateur, and heads of families.; The dispensatory of the United States of America / by Geo. B. Wood and Franklin Bache.. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
